title: How to Link Aadhaar with PAN Online — 2-Minute Process 2026
meta_description: Link Aadhaar with PAN card online 2026. ₹1,000 penalty for late linking. Step-by-step guide on incometax.gov.in. Check linking status.

hero.one_liner: Link Aadhaar with PAN in 2 minutes online — mandatory under Section 139AA. Late linking attracts ₹1,000 penalty. Unlinked PAN becomes inoperative.

sections.heading: Why Aadhaar-PAN Linking is Mandatory
content: Under Section 139AA of the Income Tax Act, linking your Aadhaar with your PAN is mandatory for all PAN holders (except NRIs and persons above 80 years of age). This rule has been in effect since 2017 with multiple deadline extensions.

What happens if you don't link: (1) Your PAN becomes 'inoperative' — you cannot file ITR, (2) You cannot open new bank accounts or demat accounts, (3) TDS is deducted at double the normal rate (source loses deduction benefit), (4) Pending tax refunds are not credited, (5) Online transactions above ₹50,000 fail, (6) Investment account opening gets blocked.
Late linking penalty: If your PAN is unlinked and you link after the deadline, a penalty of ₹1,000 applies. This fee was introduced in 2023 and continues as of 2026.
However, the penalty is waived in some circumstances — you can appeal.

sections.heading: Who Needs to Link — Quick Check

rows.label: Must Link
rows.value: All Indian residents with both Aadhaar and PAN. Non-resident Indians (NRIs) with PAN but no Aadhaar, persons above 80 years of age without Aadhaar are exempt.

rows.label: Already Linked?
rows.value: If you filed ITR in past 3 years, likely linked. If you opened PAN after 2017 and got Aadhaar, likely linked automatically. Check status before paying ₹1,000 fee.

rows.label: NRI Exception
rows.answer: NRIs don't have Aadhaar, so exempt. If NRI gets Aadhaar later, linking becomes mandatory.

sections.heading: How to Link Aadhaar with PAN — Step by Step

steps.title: Visit incometax.gov.in → Quick Links → 'Link Aadhaar'
steps.description: Go to incometax.gov.in. Look on the left sidebar or top menu for 'Link Aadhaar' or 'Link Aadhaar-PAN'. Click it. You'll see a form asking for PAN and Aadhaar.

steps.title: Enter PAN and Aadhaar numbers
steps.description: Fill in: (1) Your 10-digit PAN (e.g., ABCDE1234F), (2) Your 12-digit Aadhaar number. Ensure PAN matches your ITR PAN exactly. Aadhaar should be the active number from your Aadhaar card.

steps.title: Check name matching
steps.description: Enter your name exactly as it appears on your Aadhaar. Even minor spelling differences (e.g., 'Rajesh Kumar' vs 'Raj Kumar') will cause failure. If names don't match, you must first correct either your Aadhaar or PAN before proceeding. See FAQs for name correction process.

steps.title: Check if you need to pay ₹1,000 fee
steps.description: If linking is overdue (past the extended deadline), the portal will ask you to pay ₹1,000 late fee. If you haven't linked yet and this is your first attempt, you may not owe the fee (depending on whether deadline has passed). Pay online via 'e-Pay Tax' if required. Wait 4-5 working days for payment to reflect before trying to link again.

steps.title: Verify with Aadhaar OTP
steps.description: Click 'Validate with Aadhaar OTP'. An OTP is sent to your Aadhaar-registered mobile number. You have 5 minutes to enter the OTP. If OTP doesn't arrive: (a) Your mobile may not be registered with Aadhaar. Update at aadhar.uidai.gov.in. (b) Wait 5 minutes and request OTP again.

steps.title: Confirm linking success
steps.description: If OTP is correct, you'll see: 'Your Aadhaar has been successfully linked with PAN' + a confirmation message. Your PAN is now active and operative. No further action needed. The linking takes effect immediately.

sections.heading: How to Check If Already Linked
content: Method 1: Online Status Check — Visit incometax.gov.in → Quick Links → 'Link Aadhaar Status'. Enter your PAN and Aadhaar.
It will show: 'Linked' (green), 'Not Linked' (red), or 'Pending' (yellow).
Method 2: SMS Check — Send SMS to 567678 or 56161 in the format: UIDPAN [12-digit Aadhaar] [10-digit PAN]. You receive a reply confirming link status within 2 minutes.
Method 3: Login and Check — If you have an incometax.gov.in account, login → My Profile → Check 'Aadhaar Number' field. If it shows your Aadhaar, you're linked.

sections.heading: Why Linking Might Fail
rows: You entered wrong PAN or PAN hasn't been registered yet
Verify PAN number. New PANs take 24 hours to activate in the system. Try again next day.
Your name on Aadhaar and PAN differ (spelling, spaces, etc.)
Update name on Aadhaar (₹50, 10-30 days) OR update name on PAN (₹107, 7-15 days). Make them match first.
DOB on Aadhaar and PAN are different
Correct the discrepancy — usually Aadhaar is accurate. Update PAN with correct DOB through NSDL.
Gender field differs between Aadhaar and PAN
Correct in Aadhaar or PAN. Rarely happens but blocks linking.
Mobile not linked with Aadhaar or network issue
Link mobile with Aadhaar at aadhar.uidai.gov.in. Takes 30 minutes. Then retry.
Your PAN and Aadhaar are already linked
You don't need to link again. You're done. If needed to confirm, check status.

sections.heading: Name Mismatch Issues — How to Fix
content: Scenario 1: 'Rajesh Kumar' on Aadhaar but 'Raj Kumar' on PAN — Names must match exactly. Options: (1) Update Aadhaar to 'Raj Kumar' (simpler, faster — ₹50, takes 10-30 days), (2) Update PAN to 'Rajesh Kumar' (₹107, takes 7-15 days).
Choose based on which document is more official. Most recommend matching Aadhaar (primary ID).
Scenario 2: 'Name Surname' on Aadhaar but 'Surname Name' on PAN — Completely reversed. This often happens with traditional name orders.
Update to match — use Aadhaar as reference.

Scenario 3: After marriage, name changed but PAN still shows maiden name — Update PAN to married name (₹107, 7-15 days via NSDL). Alternatively, keep PAN in maiden name but update Aadhaar to maiden name (keep consistency).
Document update: Visit NSDL website or authorized PAN agent.
How to update Aadhaar name: Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → 'Update Aadhaar' → Pay ₹50 → Provide proof of name change (if applicable). Takes 10-30 days.
How to update PAN name: Visit NSDL website (eservices.nsdl.com) or go through your bank's PAN update service. Requires ₹107.
Takes 7-15 days.

sections.heading: What Happens After Linking
content: Immediate benefits: (1) Your PAN becomes operative, (2) You can file ITR, (3) Bank accounts accept your PAN, (4) Online transactions above ₹50,000 work, (5) TDS is deducted at normal rates (you get deduction credit).

If you had a late linking penalty: (1) ₹1,000 fee was deducted from your account. (2) Within 30 days of successful linking, your PAN status changes from 'inoperative' to 'operative'. (3) If you can't afford the ₹1,000, you can appeal within 3 months of payment with justification (hardship, error, etc.) — amount may be waived.

Refunds and ITR filing: (1) If you had pending refunds from before linking, they're unblocked within 30-60 days of linking. Monitor your account. (2) You can now file ITR without issues. (3) Past ITRs filed while unlinked are still valid, but future ITRs require linked PAN.

sections.heading: External Links & Resources
content: Official Links:
Income Tax Department — Link Aadhaar (incometax.gov.in)
Aadhaar Update Portal (myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in) — Update name, DOB, mobile.
NSDL PAN Update (eservices.nsdl.com) — Update PAN details.
Contact Support:
Income Tax Helpline: 1800-180-1961 (8 AM - 8 PM, all days).
Email: grievance@incometax.gov.in

faqs.question: I'm getting an error 'OTP not received' when trying to link. What should I do?
faqs.answer: This means your mobile number is not registered with Aadhaar. Go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → 'Update Aadhaar' → select mobile update → enter your current mobile → OTP sent to that mobile → verify → saved. Takes 30 minutes. After Aadhaar mobile is updated, try Aadhaar-PAN linking again.

faqs.question: I paid ₹1,000 late fee but linking still failed (name mismatch). Am I refunded?
faqs.answer: Not automatically. The ₹1,000 fee is processed separately from the linking. If linking fails due to name mismatch, you must correct the name first, then try linking again. The ₹1,000 you paid is held but won't be refunded immediately. Once you fix the name and successfully link, the fee is adjusted against your tax account. If you believe the fee was wrongly deducted, file an appeal within 3 months with supporting documents.

faqs.question: My PAN was issued recently (2 months ago) but linking says 'PAN not found'. Why?
faqs.answer: New PANs take 24-48 hours to appear in all government databases. Wait 2-3 days and try again. If it still doesn't work after a week, contact NSDL or visit an authorized PAN agent to confirm your PAN is active in the system.

faqs.question: I'm an NRI without Aadhaar. Do I still need to link?
faqs.answer: No, NRIs without Aadhaar are exempt from the linking requirement. However, if you get Aadhaar later (by moving back to India or applying from abroad), you must link. If you're an NRI with Aadhaar and PAN, linking is recommended for smoother tax compliance, though not strictly mandatory by current rules.

faqs.question: My Aadhaar is with one mobile, PAN is linked to a different mobile. Will linking work?
faqs.answer: Yes, it will work. The linking process uses your Aadhaar-registered mobile to send OTP. As long as your Aadhaar-registered mobile receives the OTP, linking succeeds. The mobile linked to PAN doesn't matter for this process.

faqs.question: After linking, can I unlink my Aadhaar from PAN?
faqs.answer: No, unlinking is not possible. Once linked, it remains linked permanently under current rules. You cannot have a linked-then-unlinked PAN. This is by design — the linking is meant to be permanent for tax compliance.

faqs.question: I have 3 PANs (duplicate). Do I need to link all 3 with Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: You should have only 1 PAN. If you have multiple PANs, consolidate them: (1) Contact NSDL to deactivate duplicate PANs, (2) Keep one active PAN, (3) Link the active PAN with Aadhaar. Multiple PANs cause compliance issues and can trigger IT department notices.

faqs.question: Linking shows success but portal still says 'inoperative'. When does it become operative?
faqs.answer: After successful linking, your PAN status changes to 'operative' within a few hours to 1 day. Refresh the page or check next day. If still showing inoperative after 24 hours, contact IT helpline or email grievance@incometax.gov.in with screenshot of successful linking confirmation.

disclaimer: Information is based on Income Tax Department procedures and Section 139AA as of March 2026. Deadlines, penalties, and procedures may change. Always verify current information from incometax.gov.in.
